title: The rise of political parties and the era of Jefferson
description: Test your skills on the causes and effect of policy debates in the early republic.
topic_path: The rise of political parties and the era of Jefferson | 4.2
The rise of political parties and the era of Jefferson

assessment_items.question_content:
> “The Embargo, giving time to the belligerent powers to revise their unjust proceedings and to listen to the dictates of justice, of interest and reputation, which equally urge the correction of their wrongs, has availed our country of the only honorable expedient for avoiding war: and should a repeal of these Edicts supersede the cause for it, our commercial brethren will become sensible that it has consulted their interests, however against their own will. It will be unfortunate for their country if, in the mean time, these, their expressions of impatience, should have the effect of prolonging the very suffering which have produced them, by exciting a fallacious hope that we may, under any pressure, relinquish our equal right of navigating the ocean, go to such ports only as others may prescribe, and there pay the tributary exactions they may impose. . .”

>Source: Thomas Jefferson, in a broadside signed to Eliot Brown, Jr., [UH digital history](http://www.digitalhistory.uh.edu/disp_textbook.cfm?smtID=3&psid=227), 1808

**The excerpt best reflects which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the US government’s efforts to avoid conflicts with Britain and France during the Napoleonic Wars
the Democratic-Republican party’s attempt to punish merchants who supported the Federalist party
the United States’ support for the British during the Napoleonic Wars
the federal government’s efforts to expand their borders in North America

assessment_items.question_content:
> “The Embargo, giving time to the belligerent powers to revise their unjust proceedings and to listen to the dictates of justice, of interest and reputation, which equally urge the correction of their wrongs, has availed our country of the only honorable expedient for avoiding war: and should a repeal of these Edicts supersede the cause for it, our commercial brethren will become sensible that it has consulted their interests, however against their own will. It will be unfortunate for their country if, in the mean time, these, their expressions of impatience, should have the effect of prolonging the very suffering which have produced them, by exciting a fallacious hope that we may, under any pressure, relinquish our equal right of navigating the ocean, go to such ports only as others may prescribe, and there pay the tributary exactions they may impose. . .”

>Source: Thomas Jefferson, in a broadside signed to Eliot Brown, Jr., [UH digital history](http://www.digitalhistory.uh.edu/disp_textbook.cfm?smtID=3&psid=227), 1808

**Which of the following groups would have been most likely to support Jefferson’s views expressed in this excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the Democratic-Republicans
the Federalists
farmers in the South
merchants in the North

assessment_items.question_content:
> “The Embargo, giving time to the belligerent powers to revise their unjust proceedings and to listen to the dictates of justice, of interest and reputation, which equally urge the correction of their wrongs, has availed our country of the only honorable expedient for avoiding war: and should a repeal of these Edicts supersede the cause for it, our commercial brethren will become sensible that it has consulted their interests, however against their own will. It will be unfortunate for their country if, in the mean time, these, their expressions of impatience, should have the effect of prolonging the very suffering which have produced them, by exciting a fallacious hope that we may, under any pressure, relinquish our equal right of navigating the ocean, go to such ports only as others may prescribe, and there pay the tributary exactions they may impose. . .”

>Source: Thomas Jefferson, in a broadside signed to Eliot Brown, Jr., [UH digital history](http://www.digitalhistory.uh.edu/disp_textbook.cfm?smtID=3&psid=227), 1808

**Which of the following was the most immediate result of the edict described in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: American industry suffered as Britain started substituting South American goods for American goods.
Congress repealed the Embargo Act of 1807 and trade with Britain and France increased to all-time highs during the Napoleonic Wars.
Merchants and farmers disobeyed the Embargo Act of 1807 and continued to trade with Britain and France.
France and Britain declared war on the United States and began capturing US merchant ships.

assessment_items.question_content:
> “So if a law be in opposition to the constitution; if both the law and the constitution apply to a particular case, so that the court must either decide that case conformably to the law, disregarding the constitution; or conformably to the constitution, disregarding the law; the court must determine which of these conflicting rules governs the case. This is of the very essence of judicial duty.”

> “If, then, the courts are to regard the constitution, and the constitution is superior to any ordinary act of the legislature, the constitution, and not such ordinary act, must govern the case to which they both apply.”

>Source: John Marshall, Opinion of the Court in *Marbury v. Madison,* 1803

**Which of the following was the most immediate result of the ruling excerpted?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Supreme Court demonstrated its powers as the third branch of government after establishing its power of judicial review.
The Court granted the executive branch the power to relocate indigenous nations to reservations.
The Supreme Court’s jurisdiction shrank to only cases that have gone through the entire appeals process.
The Court granted the states the power to nullify federal laws if the law gave the federal government powers not specifically assigned in the Constitution.

assessment_items.question_content:
> “So if a law be in opposition to the constitution; if both the law and the constitution apply to a particular case, so that the court must either decide that case conformably to the law, disregarding the constitution; or conformably to the constitution, disregarding the law; the court must determine which of these conflicting rules governs the case. This is of the very essence of judicial duty.”

> “If, then, the courts are to regard the constitution, and the constitution is superior to any ordinary act of the legislature, the constitution, and not such ordinary act, must govern the case to which they both apply.”

>Source: John Marshall, Opinion of the Court in *Marbury v. Madison,* 1803

**This ruling overturned which of the following laws?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the Judiciary Act of 1789
the Alien and Sedition Acts of 1798
the Northwest Ordinance of 1787
the Embargo Act of 1807

assessment_items.question_content:
> “So if a law be in opposition to the constitution; if both the law and the constitution apply to a particular case, so that the court must either decide that case conformably to the law, disregarding the constitution; or conformably to the constitution, disregarding the law; the court must determine which of these conflicting rules governs the case. This is of the very essence of judicial duty.”

> “If, then, the courts are to regard the constitution, and the constitution is superior to any ordinary act of the legislature, the constitution, and not such ordinary act, must govern the case to which they both apply.”

>Source: John Marshall, Opinion of the Court in *Marbury v. Madison,* 1803

**Which of the following statements best summarizes Marshall’s argument about the Supreme Court?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It should judge whether a law is constitutional.
It should judge whether the law or the Constitution ought to determine a case.
It should judge whether both a law and the Constitution apply to a case.
It should judge ordinary acts as equal to the Constitution.

assessment_items.question_content:
>“If the States may tax one instrument, employed by the government in the execution of its powers, they may tax any and every other instrument. They may tax the mail; they may tax the mint; they may tax patent rights; they may tax the papers of the custom-house; they may tax judicial process; they may tax all the means employed by the government, to an excess which would defeat all the ends of government. This was not intended by the American people. They did not design to make their government dependent on the States. . .”

>-Source: Chief Justice John Marshall, opinion of the Court in *McCulloch v. Maryland*, 1819

**Which of the following statements best summarizes the Court’s ruling in *McCulloch v. Maryland*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Federal laws have supremacy over state laws.
Congress only has the powers enumerated in the Constitution.
States can create “separate but equal” facilities for black people and white people.
The Supreme Court has the power to rule a law unconstitutional.

assessment_items.question_content:
>“If the States may tax one instrument, employed by the government in the execution of its powers, they may tax any and every other instrument. They may tax the mail; they may tax the mint; they may tax patent rights; they may tax the papers of the custom-house; they may tax judicial process; they may tax all the means employed by the government, to an excess which would defeat all the ends of government. This was not intended by the American people. They did not design to make their government dependent on the States. . .”

>-Source: Chief Justice John Marshall, opinion of the Court in *McCulloch v. Maryland*, 1819

**Which of the following was the most immediate result of the ruling excerpted?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Congress continued to run the Second National Bank of the United States.
The national bank’s charter ran out, and the president vetoed the new charter.
The United States suffered from an economic crisis called the Panic of 1819.
South Carolina started the Nullification Crisis, declaring the national bank was unconstitutional.

assessment_items.question_content:
>“If the States may tax one instrument, employed by the government in the execution of its powers, they may tax any and every other instrument. They may tax the mail; they may tax the mint; they may tax patent rights; they may tax the papers of the custom-house; they may tax judicial process; they may tax all the means employed by the government, to an excess which would defeat all the ends of government. This was not intended by the American people. They did not design to make their government dependent on the States. . .”

>-Source: Chief Justice John Marshall, opinion of the Court in *McCulloch v. Maryland*, 1819

**The *McCulloch* case emerged most directly from the context of which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: debates about the division of powers between the federal and state governments
critiques about the federal government’s power to enact taxes on individuals
disagreements over the role of the federal government in solving social problems
tensions between the sovereign indigenous nations and the federal government

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3ff5b86cc3e450242e830348d5b46887.svg)

**The map most directly depicts which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: expansion of land owned by the United States federal government
land ceded by Britain following its defeat in the War of 1812
increase in the number of French colonial holdings in the United States
mass relocation of indigenous nations to undesirable land in the Midwest

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3ff5b86cc3e450242e830348d5b46887.svg)

**Due to the changes shown in the map, by 1820, which of the following developments had taken place?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a rise in sectional conflicts over the expansion of slavery
a decline in foreign migration to major cities across the United States
the creation of a path to citizenship for indigenous people living in the Louisiana region
the emergence of a transcontinental railroad

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3ff5b86cc3e450242e830348d5b46887.svg)

**Which of the following historical events had a significant impact on the geographical region shown on the map above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the Missouri Compromise of 1820
the Nullification Crisis of 1832
the War of 1812
the Mexican-American War
